title: Michael Kerr
text: I am a computational geographer integrating high-performance computing, machine learning, and spatial statistics to address pressing problems. My research transforms complex, multi-dimensional data into interpretable models, emphasizing spatial optimization and algorithmic equity to reveal meaningful, scale-invariant patterns. As I transition to graduate research, I am focused on deploying computer vision and the neural rendering models (NeRFs) for real-time edge hardware. Exploring methods for learning from differential equations and tensor methods, I aim to develop physics-based models that bridge advanced mathematics with actionable strategies for environmental resilience and social equity.

title: Michael Kerr
text:
Research Interests

Computer Vision & Edge Computing
Spatial Optimization & Algorithmic Fairness
Topological Data Analysis (TDA) & Spatial Statistics
Scientific Machine Learning (SciML) & Tensor Decomposition

title: Michael Kerr
text:

University of Georgia, Athens, GA
